report_name: Anthropic's 'Mythos' AI Model Signals New Era of Autonomous Cyber Threats

threat_actors.name: Claude Mythos Preview
threat_actors.description: A frontier AI model developed by Anthropic capable of autonomously discovering novel zero-day vulnerabilities, generating functional exploit code, and executing sophisticated, multi-stage cyberattacks with minimal human intervention.
threat_actors.goals: Autonomous discovery and exploitation of software vulnerabilities.

attack_patterns.name: Active Scanning
attack_patterns.description: The Mythos AI model was described as autonomously executing reconnaissance by performing active scanning against targets.

attack_patterns.name: Exploits
attack_patterns.description: The Mythos AI model can autonomously develop and generate functional exploit code for novel zero-day vulnerabilities it discovers.

attack_patterns.name: Exploit Public-Facing Application
attack_patterns.description: The Mythos AI model can use the exploits it generates to attack public-facing applications as part of an autonomous attack chain for initial access.

attack_patterns.name: Adversarial AI Attacks
attack_patterns.description: The Mythos AI model was developed to autonomously generate its own exploits, a capability which maps to the development of adversarial AI attacks.

identities.name: Anthropic
identities.description: AI safety and research company that developed the Claude Mythos Preview model and is leading the defensive 'Project Glasswing' initiative.

identities.name: Apple
identities.description: A trusted partner of Anthropic allowed to use the Mythos model for defensive purposes under Project Glasswing.

identities.name: Goldman Sachs
identities.description: A trusted partner of Anthropic allowed to use the Mythos model for defensive purposes under Project Glasswing.

identities.name: CrowdStrike
identities.description: An AI-powered defense company mentioned as working on AI-native defense with its 'Project QuiltWorks'.
